title: sagina ciliata var. patula
intro: Sagina ciliata var. patula, also known as the fringed pearlwort, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is known for its small white flowers with fringed petals. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Sagina ciliata var. patula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote urinary health and relieve pain associated with menstrual cycles.
